mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate amplifies consumer demand and liquidity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in >55% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >60% of early-cycle observations or loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 220.0, inflation expectations rise and real yields increase, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized labor market and construction sector strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >50% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions signals manufacturing contraction and impending earnings misses, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 5-day returns in >65% of late-cycle observations or Rank IC remains below 0.01.
